hero.h1: Procurement that genuinely impacts your bottom line
hero.sub1b: It's about making better decisions — before you sit down to negotiate.
hero.sub2: We combine experience, data and tools so procurement stops being reactive and starts actively driving margin.
hero.imgAlt: Business negotiation meeting — Profitia procurement advisory

problem.h2: Procurement is no longer just an operational function

items.title: Margin impact
items.desc: Procurement decisions increasingly translate directly into profitability. Every margin point requires precision, not approximation.

items.title: Business continuity
items.desc: Supply chain disruptions have a real cost. Procurement must manage risk, not just price.

items.title: Supplier risk
items.desc: Concentration, dependency, counterparty liquidity — these are now the fundamentals of any procurement function.

items.title: Market volatility
items.desc: Commodity prices, exchange rates, availability — variables that can no longer be ignored in decision-making.

items.title: More data, less clarity
items.desc: Data is everywhere — but turning it into a concrete procurement decision requires more than Excel and intuition.

items.title: Time pressure
items.desc: Negotiations, renegotiations, RFQs — the pace of work leaves no room for the deep analysis needed most.

items.title: Growing accountability
items.desc: Procurement is responsible for more — often without adequate knowledge support or tools.

problem.quote: Procurement is becoming a strategic function — yet still operates reactively.

items.title: It's not just about negotiations
items.desc: Even the best negotiation can't replace a weak category strategy, missing data, or an unprepared team. Negotiation outcomes depend on what happens before and after.

items.title: It's not just about data
items.desc: Data without interpretation is just cost. The key is knowing what data to seek, where to find it, and how to turn it into a concrete procurement decision.

items.title: It's not just about competence
items.desc: A skilled team without the right approach and tools will still act reactively. Competence needs structure to be effective.

insight.callout2: They come from how you use it.

items.title: Advisory
items.desc: Solving specific procurement challenges and implementing recommendations in practice.

items.title: Competence
items.desc: We strengthen procurement teams so they make better decisions in real situations.

items.title: Tools
items.desc: Data and tools that turn intuition into well-founded decisions.

process.h2: What better procurement decision-making looks like

steps.title: Situation diagnosis
steps.desc: We understand the starting point — what categories, what tensions, what data you already have. We don't start from a solution in our catalogue.

steps.title: Analysis & market context
steps.desc: We identify opportunities and risks based on market data, benchmarks and procurement history. We combine external data with your internal context.

steps.title: Concrete recommendation
steps.desc: Not a report — an action plan. What to do, in what order, with what rationale. A recommendation ready to present to the board and implement with your team.

steps.title: Implementation with your team
steps.desc: We execute together with your team — so that knowledge and approach stay in the organisation. We don't do it for you; we do it with you, with knowledge transfer.

steps.title: Results verification
steps.desc: We measure what changed. Correction where needed — continuous improvement, not a one-off project with a final report.

process.footer1: It's not about how much data —

impact.h2: What changes when procurement operates strategically

cards.title: Cost control
cards.desc: You know where every cost comes from and have the arguments to negotiate or justify it.

cards.title: Less chaos
cards.desc: Instead of reacting to every change, you anticipate them. Decisions become predictable and well-grounded.

cards.title: Better team
cards.desc: Your people understand not just what to do, but why — and can independently justify their decisions.

cards.title: Consistency
cards.desc: One way of thinking about procurement across the entire organisation — instead of different approaches in every department.

cards.title: Predictability
cards.desc: Instead of supplier surprises — a renegotiation schedule, market monitoring and readiness for change.

cards.title: Real results
cards.desc: Procurement stops being a cost centre. It becomes a function that actively protects and builds margin.

usecases.h2: Situations where we help you act faster and with more confidence

items.title: Supplier price increase
items.desc: Your supplier sent a price increase notification. You have 2 weeks. Is the increase justified? How do you negotiate? What is your real bargaining power?

items.title: No benchmarks
items.desc: You don't know if you're paying the right price. You're negotiating without a reference point. Decisions are based on intuition or history, not market data.

items.title: Difficult procurement decisions
items.desc: Changing supplier, consolidating a category, make-or-buy decision. High stakes, many variables and time pressure — you need an external perspective.

items.title: Category chaos
items.desc: Every buyer operates differently. No category strategy, no consistency. It's hard to say what you're actually buying, from whom and on what terms.

proof.sectorsLabel: We have worked with organisations from the following sectors

cases.h2: What this looks like in practice
cases.imgAlt: Procurement data analysis — manufacturing company case study

blocks.label: Challenge
blocks.text: A manufacturing company with 4 key procurement categories — each managed by a different department, differently. No benchmarks, no strategy. Two key suppliers send price increases in the same quarter, totalling 14%.

blocks.label: Action
blocks.text: Category cost analysis, cross-referenced with market data, development of negotiation positions for both suppliers. A 3-week sprint: diagnosis, external data, recommendation, team preparation for the negotiations.

blocks.label: Outcome
blocks.text: Negotiations concluded at a 6% increase instead of 14% — the increase was partially absorbed. An 18-month strategy for both categories. A team equipped with a working model ready to apply independently.

cta.sub1: We can walk through one situation and show you a better approach — with no commitment and no generic proposals.
cta.sub2b: A concrete conversation about your situation.
